description_long: This house features a two-story structure with a prominent gable roof and a mix of brick and siding exterior finishes. The front facade showcases a covered porch supported by columns, brickwork around the main entry, and shuttered windows, giving it a classic suburban appearance. The rear of the house includes a large wooden deck with railings and steps, as well as multiple windows and a secondary entryway. The roof is clad in gray shingles, and a chimney is visible on one side, suggesting a fireplace inside. Windows are arranged in both symmetrical and asymmetrical patterns, with larger groupings at the back and more traditional, evenly spaced windows at the front. The overall design blends Colonial and Craftsman influences, with practical outdoor spaces and a welcoming entry.
description: A two-story house with a gable roof, brick and siding exterior, covered front porch, and a large wooden deck at the back. Features include shuttered windows, a chimney, and a mix of symmetrical and asymmetrical window arrangements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, brickwork, main entry door, and shuttered windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the siding exterior, chimney, back door, and the wraparound wooden deck with steps.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the large deck, multiple windows, secondary entry, and the extended roof overhang.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with plain siding, minimal windows, and the deck wrapping around the back.
attribute_window_arrangement: mixed symmetrical and asymmetrical rows with shuttered and large grouped windows
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with columns, brick surround, central door, and decorative shutters
